behaviors.description: The outbound classification request always contains the full allowed taxonomy label list.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (classifier module -> API client payload). MOCK-MISS: failure appears when request-building hands off to API layer, not just isolated pure logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not required; a fake client can capture payload. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is about request contents, not real network/OS effects; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model-judgment or multi-step agent pipeline is asserted.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a spy/fake API client, invoke classify(document), and assert captured request includes every taxonomy label exactly as configured (no omissions).

behaviors.description: If API returns a label outside taxonomy, module returns "unknown".
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (API response -> module validation/mapping). MOCK-MISS: bug is in handoff/response handling; deterministic stubbed API response is sufficient to expose it. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed. LLM-DEP: correctness is deterministic mapping, not model quality. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text specifies invalid-label fallback logic, which is testable with fixed stub response.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Assert classify(...) returns "unknown" and does not leak raw label.

behaviors.description: Same document yields same classification across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam (external AI output stability). LLM-DEP: this explicitly depends on real model behavior/non-determinism; stubs would always pass and hide drift/variance. MULTI-STEP: single agentic step, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text requires consistency of actual classification outcomes, not only routing/retry mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real API classifications (e.g., 20-100 trials) for identical document under production settings; assert label stability meets strict requirement (or defined tolerance if spec allows).

behaviors.description: Token usage for a typical document stays within configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (real external API token accounting/usage metering). REAL-INFRA: real API response/usage counters are needed to catch estimator-vs-provider mismatches; in-process mocks can hide real over-budget failures. LLM-DEP: not primarily output-quality dependent. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is about token consumption limits, not subjective model correctness; Integration rejected because fake usage reports can mask real provider counting behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute classification against real API with representative document(s), read actual token usage from provider response/telemetry, and assert total tokens <= configured budget per call.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit signal, module waits and retries instead of returning error.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (error handling + retry policy across classifier and API client). MOCK-MISS: needs multi-module interaction (response code/header handling + retry/backoff), but can be reproduced with deterministic fake API and controllable clock. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network not required if fake client emits rate-limit signal; (b) no AI-output quality dependence; (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text focuses on retry policy logic, not real network pathologies.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client scripted to return rate-limit error first, then success; use fake clock/sleeper to verify wait interval and retry count, and assert final result succeeds without surfacing error.
